easy.title: Basic Drag into Box
easy.description:
Create a page with 4 colored draggable squares and one large drop zone. Drag squares into the drop zone and display a count of items inside.

Hints: Use draggable='true' on squares, Make the source container also a drop zone for dragging back, Use event.dataTransfer.setData/getData to identify the dragged square

Validation: Squares drag into and out of the drop zone with visual feedback, item count updates correctly.
easy.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Basic Drag into Box</title>
  <style>
    body { font-family: system-ui; padding: 20px; }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <!-- TODO: Implement Basic Drag into Box -->
</body>
</html>
easy.solution: <!-- Solution approach for Basic Drag into Box -->

medium.title: Sortable To-Do List
medium.description:
Build a to-do list where items can be reordered by dragging, moved between 'Pending' and 'Completed' columns, and new items can be added via an input field.

Hints: Add drag handles (⠿) for better UX, Use insertBefore based on mouse Y position for reordering, Save state as JSON in localStorage on every change

Validation: Items reorder within and move between columns, new items add, counts update, data persists on reload.

hard.title: Kanban Board
hard.description:
Build a full Kanban board with columns (To Do, In Progress, Review, Done). Cards can be dragged between columns and reordered within columns. Features: add/edit/delete cards, card details, and data persistence.

Hints: Store board state as an object: { columns: { todo: [...], progress: [...] } }, Render state on every change (React-like pattern), For reordering, track drag-over item and mouse Y position, Use contenteditable for inline editing

Validation: Full Kanban functionality: drag between/reorder within columns, CRUD operations, persistence, responsive.
hard.starterCode:
